text: i was looking for some apple snacks and read the good reviews on this brand so bought a 24 pack. i just received them today opened up a package and i am enormously disappointed. they are crunchy as the package said but this is where the good points end. they do not have a very strong apple taste in fact my first thought was burned something. i dont know why but this was how it tasted to me. also after each bite there is a mildly bitter after taste. i have tried several dried apple products and have never experienced a bitter after taste until this product. i am very sorry to have wasted so much money on so many of them. for anyone who still wants to try them i will give you a tip they are sold elsewhere on amazon.com in a package of 12 instead of the 24 sold here. 11 would have been a lot easier to get rid of than 23.

pamelas gf flour products are the only gluten free flours we will use. we tried practically every other gluten free brand as well as making our own flour blend and pamelas products make the best tastingtextured gf foods. this makes great pizza crust and pie crust. we also love pamelas baking mix for pancakes waffles cookies donuts cakes etc. you can find tons of recipes for the baking and bread flours on the pamelas website.

i grew up in england eating kippers and if fresh ones were unavailable john west was the thing to have.these days i reside in the usa i have tried several different brands as i found them in local stores that heralded from all over the world but nothing comes close to john west kippers.john west has such consistently high quality standards and the product is flavorful and falls apart in the mouth a culinary delight.i was so pleased when i found them on amazon!

the first time i had a terrys chocolate orange was during christmas when our local superstore usa stocked them. i love chocolate. and orangeflavored things are among my favorite flavors. i never once imagined that combining the two flavors would yield one of my favorite desserts in the world!the folks in england got it right when they began to make terrys chocolate oranges! buying these in bulk are totally worth the price you pay for them they are meant to be savored. one orange can last you a month if you really really try but they can also disappear in just a few hours if you arent careful!they might look like theyre pretty delicate but trust the label when you read whack and unwrap! take the ball in your hand and turn the orange so the bottom of it is facing downward find a sturdy surface like a kitchen countertop then raise the orange up high and whack it down hard onto the flat surface. youll feel the individual slices form and then you can unwrap pick out a slice and savor its delicacies!buy them today so this can remain a staple in amazons food store! i dont want them to disappear anytime soon! im going to order another case in a few weeks!1000000000000000 stars if possible!!!
summary: terrys milk chocolate oranges are a treat not to be missed!

yes people will tease you when you talk about loving microwave pork rind but theyre tasty enough to endure it. the directions on the bag are fine but not the way i do it. put a heaping 13 of a cup of the greasy pellets in a brown paper lunch bag. lay the bag on its side in the microwave. now its time to get to know your microwave. for mine it takes 2 and a half minutes for all of the pellets to pop perfectly. if you still have unpopped pellets try a little longer. too long and theyll taste a little burnt. amazon is wonderful as always to offer them cheaper and faster than anywhere else! enjoy!

text: i actually order these and give them to give my grandmother who lives abroad. shes a diabetic and is always wanting more sugarfree candy. she has them everyday as a treat between meals. ive already ordered the pack of 12 twice which is a good sign because she still hasnt gotten sick of them!! she likes that they are sour and that it includes two different flavors. shes not super picky but there are definately a bunch of sugarfree options that she does not like at all so i guess they are pretty good and the price here is great.

i bought this tree and didnt know what to expect from the reviews. it arrived today in seemingly good health no brown spots however a very small amount of dead foliage in the substrate which i promptly removed. shipping was fast 3 days from cali to pa and the tree was packed very nicely in such a way that it couldnt accidentally fall out of its pot. mine looks to be about 67 inches tall lots of little branches and nice green foliage will probably have to restyle this spring however all in all i think this was a good purchase.i would definitely recommend this to anyone looking to start bonsai. will be a welcome addition to my front porch.

i have not purchased this item from amazon but we use it all the time purchasing from a local grocery for any meat being cooked. great for seasoning catfish prior to frying. the bottle description says we created our texjoy steak seasoning for steaks and hamburgers but taste how it adds joy to chicken seafood salads vegetables pork pasta french fries and even popcorn. our vice president loves it on tart apples. texjoy products are made in beaumont texas.

today is june 29th and i began this adventure on march 1st 4 months ago and the production expectancy for this set. the sprouting was slow but the cs rep from aerogrow suggested i be patient. he said they would really take off once they sprouted. well they did and we were off to the races. so far 1 mini jalapeno and 1 purple super hot have bit the dust completely. the second mini jalapeno grew huge but never produced a single pepper. two of the purple super hots are still alive but have never even produced a flower either. both of the red fires are lush and have produced a total of 10 peppers so far. considering the cost of electricity the kit the extra nutrients which needed to be purchased because there are not enough included the replacement light kit and the hours upon hours of talking to touching fertilizing by movement and a tiny hand held fan csrs suggestion these are the most expensive peppers ive ever consumed. im thinking of transplanting these into my garden plot to see if they ever produce. i have one more seed kit herbs which im going to give a try and then im going to give up on this and im not going to buy another set of bulbs even though its advised. if you want alot of leaves which you do not consume then this is the seed kit for you. i on the other hand wanted something to use in the kitchen.
summary: chili peppers seed kit not very fruitful

text: it is now 2012 i will leave my original review below but need to note that the last time i purchased the roland peppers i was very disappointed because they were on the mushy side. not totally mushy but definitely not as firm as they were a couple years ago. things do change over time. and amazon has the delallo brand in oil with garlic in stock again so that is what we are eating.here is the original review i wrote in 2009these are the second best roasted red peppers ive purchased. the best ones were delallo lightly marinated in olive oil and whole garlic....but i cant find that particular brand anymore.the texture is very firm and the cans are packed quite full so you really get your moneys worth. you can even divide the large can and put portions in small freezer containers and they are just as good after being frozen and then thawed. i use red peppers in a lot of salads and spreads because we dont eat tomatoes and i will definitely purchase these again.the only thing i would criticize is that there is a bit too much of the charred skin on the peppers but it is not too difficult to clean off and you expect some of that when you get roasted peppers.
summary: nice firm peppers in the past not so firm now

first off these are skinny skinny noodles more slender than normal wheat vermicelli and as such much more delicate. the bag they came in did nothing to keep them from shattering in shipping and every time i picked up the package i felt like i was breaking them more.definitely read the directions when you go to prepare them. they say to soak for one minute and then basically just heat them up in whatever dish youre making. they need pretty much zero cooking so would be great for making fast or onthego meals.but for dinners where you are home and happy to wait other rice noodles are better. these have a slightly dingy flavor probably from the fact that theyre made of brown rice and get soggy and pasty super fast. they ended up soaking up so much water that they tasted watery and made my asianinspired sauce taste bland. im used to cooking asian rice noodles since we try to eat glutenfree and while these arent the worst i have tried id prefer most brands over this one.ill give it three stars for the fact that if i were to make my own instant ramen to take somewhere this would def. be the brand id choose as they dont really need to cook like other brands do.
summary: tastes vaguely of cardboard and the noodles went soggy fast

text: as we all know.....any canned soup organic or not should be avoided or at least limited. it does work for storm supplies due to its easy medium term storage and it being infrequently or never used. it will end up in a hunting camp for a few days of quick meals toward the end of its shelf life. everything tastes better in fresh air and this stuff isnt bad. again its use is very limited.dont like the bpa issue either. i did speak to wolfgang puck about pba and their plans. like most they are treading water to see what the government friends of the fda does or does not do about it. whats a little more poison added to the current mix in processed foods.ever notice that most of the wolfgang puck soup ingredients here on amazon end at contains 2 or less of the following........?as i indicated above we did try this soup and it tasted ok nothing special. try adding a little more sea salt. it is better than the msg enhanced preservative laden campbells brand crap a brand we would not buy regardless of circumstance. one never knows who actually made this soup.we consider the organic versions the lesser of insert remaining cliche and have a case......in case.

june 28 2011. the expiration date. ordered jan 17 delivered jan 21st. the product is really dry. drier than usual. i mean yes these bars are really crunchy but the oats and honey that came in the variety pack i also bought wasnt as dry as these. part of me wonders if these have been on the shelf for too long and amazon decided to put them on a subscribe and save sale to rotate the stock.i bought these or emergency supplies in case of a natural disaster or something but now i see ill have to eat them all in 5 months. thats pretty bad to me.yes people say they seem to love these and they cant find them on store shelves and i guess in that case its great. but i do want to question how old these are as some products i see expiring two years about in the future. so just buyer beware in the case that you are the sole consumer and arent part of a larger group who might eat these.

i have two tiny dogs 5 lbs. and one 78lb. standard poodle. they all love the evo beef canned food. we mix it with beef small bites evo beef dry food for the little guys one of them has a mild chicken allergy and large bites poultry evo dry for the big guy. i feel their nutrition is really good regular uncomplicated. easy to clean up after. they also eat the evo beef wild cravings treats when we treat. carbs and grain such as that found in people food are anathema for our dogs. makes them sick gassy and even have diarrhea. we really try to avoid giving any scraps. just dont overfeed with the evo. its super concentrated so take your dogs activity level into account. when my walking time decreased bc of the extended heat wave my poodle soon gained a few pounds he didnt need. i cut back a little and he is evening out. you pay more but life is simple and my dogs absolutely love this food.
summary: my 3 dogs love this food. healthy and happy.

i am sorry but this is not authentic dutch licorice. the original dutch licorice known in holland as drop is made with arabic gum. however gustafs is made with wheat starch instead probably because it is much cheaper. that does affect the taste as wheat starch has a more sticky pasty texture where arabic gum has a firmer and smoother texture.nevertheless this is by far the best drop i have found so far that is readily available in the usa for a decent price. importing the real stuff can get quite expensive. so i will be buying this again.
summary: not the real thing but still pretty good.

we purchased the item from costco and have enjoyed the snack without reading about its origin individual bars have a lot of attractive information but does not have country of origin. to replenish our supply i decided to order it from amazon and for the first time after reading the reviews became aware that the food product was made in china. what guarantee is there that the product is gmo free? are random samples of the food item tested for gmo presence? is the product manufactured in a facility that does not process nonvegan items? who has tested food products from china for presence of heavy metals and other contaminants and is there a continuous testing? the claim is that the product is all natural but who has verified that components used are grown on land that is not contaminated with chemicals and heavy metals. the product is very tasty and we have not suffered from food poisoning but now that i am aware of the country of origin i have grave doubts about the integrity of the product for long term use bioaccumulation of contaminants and cumulative effects over time and will look for similar snacks that are made locally made in usa. i have no confidence in food products from china based on available evidence of widespread pollution and contaminated pet food ingredients and baby food.

yes manuka honey is special. it is cultivated only in new zealand and southeast australia. it has also been used by some researchers in a clinical trial as a medicinal honey to ameliorate some of the ill effects of cancer treatment. it probably works in some medical indications. but this is not because it has magic powers or that it is vastly different from other honeys. manuka honey is used as a medicinal honey rather than other honeys because it is very well characterized chemically. that characterization is consistent because manuka honey comes from only two very similar flowers. this honey is unusually viscous and it has relatively high concentrations of the chemicals that kill bacteria in honey. but most good honeys are viscous and have antibacterial potency they are just not as well characterized for medicinal purposes. as far as using it as a folk remedy for all these various conditions? honey has been used as a folk medicine for centuries and it works for some things. as a toxicologist i have to say well maybe it will work for this or that but we cannot really know if something works medically until we do a clinical trial. the reported cures are possibly real and are probably the result of immune stimulation or antibacterial activity that might occur with any honey. but for most people it is really all about how the honey tastes unless you are on a clinical trial.i think it tastes rather good. it is floral and complex and full of flavor. its viscosity means it clings to your hot biscuit better than most and it comes in a fancy jar. but my wife did not like it. it was not clear but rather cloudy like honey that has begun to precipitate out its sugar. but that was not the source of the cloudiness it was actually due to the presence of honey comb. and the honey tastes of comb which my wife found a bit objectionable.so why buy this honey. i really cannot think of a reason unless you are trying to impress someone. i have had much better much clearer honey from a roadside stand in north carolina particularly sourwood. and this stuff is terribly expensive. i do not see anything wrong with trying it on your warts but you might try a cheaper honey first. yeah but there is that impress people thing.
summary: yeah yeah miracle drug but how does it taste and is it worth the price

in case anyones confused this is 96 oz total per order. you get two double pack boxes.as for the cereal i think i got a bad batch aug2412 p23. a 14 oz box i got from the local grocery store with a oct2011 best by date tasted way betterlight crisp flakes mixed with thin crunchy ones with sweet clusters of oats that give it a granolalike taste and texture. the oats from this batch were much different some seemed whole still instead of rolled making it harder chewier and nuttier some were giant toasted brown clusters of oats toasted stiff and hard. not exactly bad but the first bowl was quite a surprise due to how the oats would be rolling around in my mouth forcing me to chew much more. the whole oats were like bigger sunflower seed kernels but thicker and harder.update opened another box after finishing the other 14 oz boxes and definitely taste something funny in this. oats werent really hard this time but they had a taste like some sort of chemical cleaner or pesticide in it. i wish i could return this. i sense its a health hazard. i wonder if i could send this in to test somewhere. to top it all off i found ants had infested a box. there doesnt seem to be any doubts that theres some quality control issue going on here. maybe the chemical taste was some ant killer chemical and the production plant this originated from had some sort of ant infestation and they sent some tainted product out.

text: i have subscribe to these to get that extra discount which makes them a not bad price compared to others one cups. as most others have pointed out this is not a true kcup as the base in not plastic. these make a nice strength of coffee for your medium cup size. it is a little weak coffee for me on the larger size. they come in three bags and i do wish that they could be resealed but i solved the problem by placing them in an air tight container. when i opened the bag of coffee i was hit by the smell of fresh ground coffee. i hadnt realized how much i have missed that smell. you dont get that with the plastic cups. over all i thought it was a good price and makes a nice cup of coffee. i will be keeping my subscription.

i fed this product to my cat and my roommates cat. i was experimenting with food since the cats are almost 1 and i wanted to introduce different kinds of adult food so theyd get used to them. i mixed half science diet dry kitten food with this product and the cats began to eat less. i was worried about how much they were eating nearly 14 their original diet so i filled one bowl with this indoor cat food and one bowl with their kitten food. they barely touched the indoor cat food while eating most of their kitten food. i ended up having to mix some canned cat food in with it to get them to eat.part of it might be that the food is triangular and much larger than the kitten food. they had a hard time eating it at first since they were used to the small pieces of the kitten food.dont be discouraged though. my cat cant tell the difference between the kitten food and the regular adult food. they really enjoy science diets fish flavored food so give that a try.

when i adopted my dog he had a rash all over his body and big hairless patches. he is a white pitbull with very short fur so he was pink and raw all over. i switched him to this food and after several months his coat grew in thick and soft everywhere even the bald patches. the rash gradually disappeared. i tried switching him to a cheaper ricelamb food but the rash immediately came back within a couple weeks. i put him back on the grainfree natures recipe food and the rash went away again. definitely a great food for dogs with sensitive skinfood allergies. the only thing i dont like is the cost but all grainfree foods cost about the same and this is actually the cheapest one at the pet store i go to. my dog is a 100 happy equalopportunity lover of everyone and he loves this dog food too.
summary: greatly improved my dogs skin and fur

im not a coffee connoisseur at all and cant tell the difference between harbucks or mcdowels but i do know the taste of ca phe sua da in socal and have been casually engaged in finding the right blend. the oft recommended cafe du monde is definitely not the flavor. im not sure what chicory is but its a funny tasting coffee fairly mild and no matter how long i try to filter it to get it strongerdarker the coffee just isnt the right amount of blackness. so i turned to trung nguyen and based on recommendations from internet friends and the website itself this is one of if not the most popular blend. its got a great flavor full of nuttiness and sure presses better than du monde almost no grounds at bottom of cup but its still not the ca phe sua da im used to. for one the color is a definite brown not black and it isnt strong. ill give the creative 1 a try which says its a dark and strong robusta coffee no blend compared to this which is a blend of nearly everything.so if you are looking for a great tasting coffee full of complex flavors that are beyond what i can describe give this a try... you wont be disappointed to the least. but if you are looking for that really strong jet black coffee that can strip paint off a car and wake up dirt and that when mixed with condensed milk turns into a rich goldenbrown concoction that is ca phe sua da then you need to look elsewhere like that creative 1... possibly.
summary: great coffee but not the flavor im looking for for ca phe sua da

i am a big fan of suave and since this product is made by the same company i jumped at the chance to try something new from them. i have to say my review may be tainted by the way the product came to me. first when the package arrived it was leaking. the bottle was inside a plastic bag which must have been made to seal in any leakage. i dont know if it was broken or the button on top somehow got pushed but half of the bottle of shampoo was out. once i cut off the wrapping and threw away the spoils the bottle seemed okay. i put it in my shower eager to try it the next morning.the shampoo itself is okay. it smells and feels like the color seal shampoo you get in a hairdye box for after rinsing. im not found of that sort of smell everyday but its not unpleasant. after using the shampoo for a couple of days my hair felt limp and oily compared to using suave shampoo and conditioner i like to use mango mandarin citrus smoothie or apple. for someone whos hair is usually light healthy and shiny i did not like the effect this shampoo gave to my hair. for someone who tends to have dry scalp it might help you. i stopped using it after a week.after a few uses the bottle started leaking from the almost invisible seam holding the lid on. i dont know if i got a messed up bottle or if this is how they all are. i would not buy this again.
summary: not as great as i hoped.

text: im not much of a coffee drinker. ive had a few cold coffee drinks here and there but i love coffee ice cream and candies. i decided to give this cappuccino drink by illy issimo a chance since ive been trying a few new things lately like how i had doritos for the first time last week in my nearly 27 years of existence. my initial reaction was that it didnt taste very good but again thats probably because i dont drink coffee very much. but after a few sips i got used to it. im not saying it tasted good by any means but its certainly not the worst thing ive drank this year. coffee fans will either love or hate this depending on their personal preferences.the nutrition info listed on the can is a bit reassuring since this drink isnt that bad for you and you get a little bit of protein too. its fairly low in sugar for a drink like this too. and while its not advertised as an energy drink i actually felt upbeat for an hour or two after finishing the can...something i cant say for other drinks ive had like mountain dew or jarritos.im giving it 3 stars because its just plain ok in my book. nothing special but not terrible. its still weird drinking coffee from a can though. would i recommend it to anyone? probably not unless they could only choose between this and diet pepsi.

terrible.where to begin first a list of ingredientsmineral oil hyrogenated coconut oil hyrogenerated styrenebutadiene copolymer palm oil fragrance bht shea butter trticum vulare wheatgerm oil sweet almond oil sunflower seed oil soybean oil argania spinosa kernal oil last but not least aloe leaf juice.there is no possible way this product is going to provides light nongreasy nourishment to scalp hair yet that is exactly what is says on the bottle.second the instructions for use are hip but vague 1. cleanse 2. smooth. 3. sooth 4. seal.i went online for further clarification and found nothing but a spectacular ad campaign with a beautiful model and her amazing hair. so i decided to experiment i figured my first use would be the same way one would use v05 hair oil a great product by the way. i would wash my hair forgo conditioner rub in the oil and rinse. if this was too light then i would on my second application wash rub in oil and leave it in.i never got to the second experiment.after only washing my hair applying a dose of no more than a teaspoon seriously a little goes a long way. and then giving my hair a rinse and patted my hair out with a towel. one and a half hours later my hair is fairly thick goes to the middle of my back. my hair was dry but appeared matted greasy to the eye as well as to the touch. i looked like i had laquered my head! i could not wash my hair fast enough. oddly upon my second hairwashing my hair had achieved a pleasant level of softness shine and a visible improvement in my split ends. but at a very gross price.v05 is by far a less troublesome and superior product. i believe ill stick with that and avoid this one and i can not recommend this product as nothing more than a great lubricant for perhaps sliding a tight ring off a swollen finger.
summary: greasy stiff could not get it out of my hair fast enough!

basically for baking andor diabetics. can be replaced by less expensive brands that achieve the same results.

i love my vegemite but then i am a kiwi and grew up as a vegemite kid. i eat it on toast crackers use it in gravy as a flavour boost i put it in stews for the same reason. its great on baked potatoes with a dollop of butter... remembering that in the downunder way you use only a small amount of it as its very strong. i recall as a kid having it on sandwiches cheese and vegemite cheese lettuce and vegemite. vegemite and chopped walnuts on bread. grilled cheese and vegemite on and on it is so multi useful. dry toast it on bread sticks for teething babies... i am nearly out of my current vegemite having shared it to some adventuring americans here a work for the record i got no converts but one of the drs assured me i would find it on amazon so here i am looking and yayyyy i dont have to be deprived. i read another review and here is my absolute favourite way to have vegemite.. thick slice of fresh bread thick load of butter and a small portion of vegemite.. scrape on and scrape off.. just like the other reviewer said and whammo a great feast !!! thank you amazon !!!

the description of the product says breakfast blend so i expected something that would actually provide a bit of a caffine wakeup... but when you actually receive the box you find out its also described as mild. had i known this i never would have ordered this to start with. when you actually taste it you find out mild is a vast overstatement the coffee tastes like warm brown water with no character whatsoever. since none of the 47 kcups remaining there are 3 coffee drinkers here all agree its not worth a second cup cant be returned guess ill save it for company who really dont want any coffee but are interested in how the brewer itself works. then well throw out the end product.

this kcup saved my kcup maker from extinction. i was used to dark strong brewed coffee and none of the kcups i tried up to this one could provide that. jet fuel is strong and dark and has a slightly sweet aftertaste and is not at all bitter. it is awesome.
summary: thats what i am talking about!

text: always wanted to make my own hummus and eggplant dip this brand of tahini id never tried before or seen in supermarkets so i decided to order and try it....excellent by itself or in recipes! highly recommended.
summary: excellent by itself or in hummusbaba ganoush

text: these were much talked about during a recent party we hosted. everybody wanted to know where we got these so i hope i brought amazon more business. the 4 star rating is because some people complained that the chocolates were hard to unwrap otherwise they tasted great.

i bought this to use in my cats automatic feeder during a weekend trip. i had a hard time loading it into the feeder because my kitten was trying to eat the food as i was doing it! its pure meat no fillers which i love. i also use it as a special treat for my boy. my only concern with using it as a meal replacement is that it doesnt provide the moisture and vitamins that cats need but for a day or two i think its great.

wont be reordering. not a fan of the taste. they are drier than a raisin or dried cranberry. the flavor is almost like a cranberry meets raisin but dry.i keep offering them to people to get rid of them. no one would take 2nds.i tried mixing them in my greek yogurt. theyd soften up a bit but the flavor was still meh.

this is pretty good but it tasted like a slightly salted dulce de leche which costs a fraction of the price. to me it did not have a true salted butter caramel flavor such as youd find in bequet celtic sea salt caramels which i melt down with a bit of water to make a sauce and save my teeth....
summary: pretty good but not worth the price

text: quite disappointed. the pills dont last that long it wears off after a few tries per pill and not all the food items on the suggested list works. e.g. cheese. maybe it was a bad batch but im not taking the risk to purchase it again.

our two greyhounds love the greenies dental chews. i prefer to buy them the smaller treats since they are both seniors now and dont like to chew on things for extended periods of time. the regular size fits that need.

i had purchased the product before from local supermarket but have had a hard time locating was glad to find it on amazon but when received all 6 jars show a best used by may 2011 a 2 month period. much shorter than i would have expected for a lot. will try and find a local supplier to avoid this problem in the future.

as the title states the main fault with this oatmeal is the artificial maple flavor. its slight but leaves an odd taste in my mouth. i mean oatmeal is healthy and good in itself why add that extra artificial sugar if it detracts from the overall taste of the food? in this case brown sugar would have been enough. or at least use real maple syrup.texture and consistency is up to you based on how much water you put in and how long you cook it. i put in different amounts of water and i thought the oatmeal tasted best with more water. my guess is that the water diluted the strong sugary taste so that it was more palatable to me. i still give this product 4 stars because most flavored instant oatmeal have this issue of using artificial ingredients that affect the taste.ultimately after trying different kinds of flavored instant oatmeal from various companies i think i enjoy plain oatmeal with dried or fresh berries the most. healthy and good what more can you ask?
summary: slightly artificial flavor but still eatable.

text: the cost breaks down to a dollare and oz and if your like me and love hops their is no guilt in adding more and dry hoppinig later... go head and buy a few packs and save money down the line..

i enjoy several different varieties of kashi snack bars but i really expect something that is not only made with fresh ingredients to taste like the ingredients they are made with. these are very dry and have minimal flavor so i will eat the rest of them but will not reorder.
summary: dry and tasteless sums it up

text: these cookies are surprisingly good! i havent had a cookie that i liked this much since pamelas lemon shortbread.i took 2 of these cookies and put a marshmallow on one and a sprinkle of chocolate chips on the other and microwaved for 15 seconds. then i made them into smores. my kids have a ton of food allergies so this was a huge hit!

the product was exactly what we were looking for. the bags are stuffed very full so you feel youre getting a good deal. the puffs are large and look appetizing. taste is good. we actually purchased these for our food challenged dog prescribed by vet....but they are good enough that we can eat them too.
summary: food for dog that people want to eat too

text: this is a great tasting bar with an unusually different combination of dates and walnuts. i find it to be a substitute for a candy bar or sweet bite. however in my opinion its far too sweet. my bars have crystallized sugar in them. dont know if thats intentional or not. perhaps the bars are old? however i find it to be a it hurts my teeth kind of sweet ... yes for sensitive areas on my teeth its far too sugary. in addition i highly doubt the caloric content ... 150 calories for 1.4 ounce bar with dates walnuts honey glucose as the first 4 ingredients. given the sugar ingredients density caloric content of comparable bars are in the 250range. i am a lifetime weight watcher and extraordinarily conscientious about these things. the listed caloric content is too low for the ingredients and sugar.i wont be getting these bars again.

having had plantain chips before i knew them to be somewhat salty but was not ready for the new level. the nutrition facts state that there are 30mg of sodium per 30g of chips 11000th of a gram per each gram of chip which doesnt sound like all that much.however i had to toss the content of the last bag of chifles plantain chips with garlic into a colander with paper towels and shake them vigorously over the kitchen sink for several minutes before i could attempt to eat these chips. this process eliminated much of the excess seasoning leaving the paper colander and sink heavily coated in seasoning but the chips were still too salty to enjoy.i really like garlicflavored plantain chips so hope that this was just a matter of a bad batch although even other bags were saltier than id like. i will keep an eye out for a low sodium version of chifles brand and recommend trying a different brand of plantain chips. please note that i did not buy this particular bag here but have had these chips on several occasions.

i am from a country where coconuts are the thing. p we have them on the streets even on the backyard pretty much all year long. i am now in the us where a fresh conconut is not the same . so when i saw these chips in the storetjmax i was pretty intrigued. i cannot believe how good they are. they taste just like coconut not overly sweet but just like a coconut tastes like! i cannot wait to get moreeee!!!

chuckles were a childhood treat and my favorite candy. however chuckles seem to be an east coastmid west product. i never could find them in the west or the rocky mountain area.then i found them on line and at amazon. i have now ordered chuckles from amazon twice. each time they have been soft and fresh.treat yourself to a great candy.

i have to disagree with the 1 star reviews. used on its own this syrup is ok but you need to experiment to get the right mix. the ideal mixture for a large latte is to use just one tablespoon of this syrup with a dash of ground cinnamon. i think the 1 star people are using way too much.

im no baker and this was relatively easy to make. the cookies tasted fine i guess but they were too soft for me. i dont know why i thought gingerbreadginger snaps but my family loved them. the icing was not very good. we went out and got our own. it was just powdered sugar and there was way too little to do anything w it.
this will probably last us as long as we have the machine. lol for home use theres a lot but it makes it taste like the fair ground popcorn. love it.

honey stinger waffles cant be beat for a reasonably low cal pick me up. they are my favorite in between in between pro bars and lara bars. my only criticism is amazon does not reliably keep them in stock. love organic honey stinger waffles.
it was a great produce the only problem that i had with it was that i did not think that it had any calories in it but it did

i was very happy with how much one package made! i made more than 8 patty shapes and had plenty left over that i broke up for nachos and spaghetti sauce. the nice mellow spices in this mix worked well with both.great product to use as a meat substitute!
this is the best coffee ive ever had...that is for sure..my hubby n i simply fell in love with dark roast!....would love to try some more!..thanks a ton brooklyn bean!!!!...u rock!!!

i liked the flavor and the texture but my husband didnt. he liked the flavor but called it mush because it was too soft and every time we ate it we had to wash our hands afterwards because it kept falling apart. i will have to find another product because of it
this coffee we have purchased many times from amazon and each time it has met all of our expectations. we will purchase it again

so far ive tried all of the flavors and while there are some im not over the hill about ive had pretty good experiences with them.this is one of the flavors id purchase again. im a big fan of anything lemon or lime flavored so i had to give this a try. ill admit up front that i havent had a key lime pie for years so i dont remember the flavor like it was yesterday and this probably worked in my flavor. i remember it in general though.this tastes awesome. i can definitely taste the lime and it tastes like what i remember a key lime pie to be. the lime flavor is there but its backed up by a nice creamy taste that almost made me want to grab a few extra slices of gum. hey its 15 calories a slice so i can splurge on this while i wouldnt want to splurge with the actual pie. unfortunately it falls prey to the same problem that the equally yummy apple pie flavor had the flavor doesnt last nearly long enough.ill absolutely buy this again despite the flavor only lasting 12 minutes.
i bought 2 jack links beef steak pack of 12. one of the two packs was open and had only 11 beef steaks. also the meat is very tough to chew unlike when you buy from grocery stores.

i was surprised that this sauce had flavor. even the few clams that were in it were not overcooked and chewy like most made in the usa sauces are. still for the price here on subscribe and save it comes out pretty reasonable. i can still remember when i was younger that this sauce was imported from italy. not anymore. so if you can doctor it up a bit with your own fresh spices this is not bad as a base for your pasta sauce.

ive been suckered into buying numerous bags of these haribo candies damn you gold box and these cherries were among the most tasty. very dense take quite awhile to chew. very nice fruity flavor. a recommended stop on the road to obesity. and you can say goodbye to your pancreas on the way. but...its so worth it.

i heard about zico chocolate a few months ago and was extremely skeptical. however i was delightfully surprised by the taste... and am now hooked! i cant get enough! i literally drink it every morning for breakfast. it tastes just like chocolate milk but has all of the health benefits of coconut water. way to go zico you knocked this one out of the water!

im a diehard fan of liquid sucralose liquid splenda like ezsweetz but i heard that erythritol is a much better sweetener for chocolate recipes. ive used truvia in three different recipes with cocoa unsweetened chocolate and apples and it improved the flavor dramatically. i will be using truvia in recipes where granular sweetener is required and to counteract the aftertaste when using lots of suralose. a 12 or 11 ratio of truvia to liquid sucralose works really well. it also saves money since truvia is more expensive.
i mix this dog food with prairie salmon and they love it. their coats are slick and shiny even this time of year when they tend to get shaggy. i have dobermans.

when i opened the medium sized packing box i found a fruitcake about the size of a 3x3x3cube. i first burst out laughing at the size and then i looked at the product box as this wasnt what i expected a pound fruitcake to look like! its very dense with lots of nuts and moist fruit but it is a bit difficult to cut in spots. ive loved fruitcake for over 50 years but i think fruitcake is very subjective just like pizza. i wouldnt order it again as i like mine with less densely packed nuts. theres nothing wrong with the taste and it does have more delicious cherries in it than most cakes.

i purchased this one and the 80s one and they basically had the same candy in each. there were some differences but were missing popular candy that we remembered at each time.the amount of candy and the freshness were great but for the price of the box and shipping i could have put something cheaper together myself.it was a cool concept and gift but disappointing when opened.
summary: good amount but missing key items

text: i tried this product for only 2 weeks and i feel so energetic. instead of once a month now i have sex with my wife 2 times a week and my wife is alot happier. i totally recommend this product!!

im originally from hawaii and now live in no. california. no one knows about poke unless theyve been to the islands. ive made this mix with ahi and salmon and it hit the spot!!!. i also added some green seaweed which made it even better. it truly is a taste of home for me and at least i can order this mix when im feeling homesick. it was so easy to prepare. im glad its available on amazon. thanks!!!

45.99 for sugar free cider? are you kidding me? better off finding it at a grocery store during holiday season and stock up! it costs around 4.00 for a 10 pack. dont waste your money with these rip off sellers.

i found this item cheaper at local stores when i could find it. shipping costs are too expensive for an item like this. i wish they were easier to find at stores. in addition i wasnt all that satified with the taste of the coffee.

this coffee is delicious. i was able to get it when there was a coupon so the price was amazing. regardless its a delicious fair trade organic coffee. it seems to be mostly a medium roast with rich flavor and lots of aroma. i used it for manual drip coffee and was very happy with it.

i regularly order chukars pie fillings and toppings because i just cant make anything that compares! the raspberry cherry preserves are very well balanced with a not too tart and not too sweet flavor. i use this and their other toppings to make crumbles crisps and also as toppings on ice cream. each holiday season we stock up on these for a special treat. they are also perfect gift items as they are far superior to any variety of topping or preserve that youre going to find in the grocery store. you just cant beat pacific northwestern cherries!
i absolutely love these sunflower seeds. the bacon flavoring takes them over the top for sunflower seeds! amazing! my baby bought these for my birthday to my utter surprise. best gift i received!
it tastes really well. it is my first time eating this brank. i think it is hard comparing to other chocolate that i normally eat but the flavor is good different.

hands down nong shim produces a quick snackmeal. after working aboard vessels on the bering for 12 years ive come to the conclusion that this little bowl has become the epitome for a meal if you dont have time want to pass on the food offered in the galley or trying to survive during the down time when the catch has run dry and boredom sets in causing the munchies.ive seen lots of combos with this onevienna sausages spam albacore tuna sardines chilies three crabs maggi peanut butter cumin and beans...sodiumyes its packed with a wallop of the stuff but when youre grinding 12 to 16 a day you burn it off pretty fast. for those worrying about msg then just set the spice pack aside and work the noodles with the condiment of choice to your liking.the noodles hold up to a hot broth and dont get soft like a lot of the other brands. prep time is 2 minutes. inexpensive and a good quick meal if you treat it right.
summary: the preferred noodle of the bering sea fleet

text: ive finally found a chew toy that can keep my hyperactive dog entertained and distracted for more than 10 minutes. ive tried everything she had no interest in hooves pigs ears rarely lasted more than a few minutes and synthetic toys couldnt get even the slightest bit of attention from her. these are absolutely great! she has finally stopped chewing on my childrens toys! they do smell rather horrible while she chews on them but they get the job done.
summary: longest lasting chew toy ive found

text: everything was fine as far as the price and convenient free delivery. no dented cans and the product tastes good. the only issue i have is that their stock is not as new as what you get in the stores. the shelf life on cans i bought two months ago at a local grocery store expire nine months after the ones i bought from amazon just last week.if you store food as we do and need optimum shelf life then you are better off going to your local grocery store for a fresher product.
summary: shelf life much shorter than buying at a local store

text: they wont taste the same as other gummies. and they were less chewy than other gummies like albanese best tasting ones ive found or haribo. chewiness is one of elements i love about gummies so i will not purchase yummy earth again. but if you are really looking for a healthier substitute judge for yourself.
summary: organic so of course you sacrifice taste

text: love this stuff its my favorite seasoning for french fries! unfortunately its hard to find near me and when i can find it i only can find the small jar which never lasts long. finding a huge canister like this on amazon was just the thing. can now use it on everything without fear of running out... try it on your fries.. trust me!
summary: love this stuff.. hard to find near me

text: standard disclaimer newmans own organics ginger snaps were obtained via the amazon vine program.ok my wife and i tried these ginger snaps and came to the same conclusion theyre spicy and hard.the ginger is pronounced. really pronounced. you have to eat this with a cup of tea or a glass of milk to cut the sharpness. another reason to have liquids on hand these little suckers are hard. serious crunch going on here. however a dunk in the aforementioned tea will soften them right up.i dig ginger snaps with a hot cup of tea so youd think these would be right up my alley. but the spice is a touch too harsh and the hardness of the cookie takes a serious dunk to get over. still i can deal. especially on a cold day.however if youre looking for a snack cookie you can munch right out of the bag these are not for you.
this item is wonderful and it arrived in like no time at all !!! thanks so much i will be ordering again soon because everyone loves these!!!!!
this product is old. the experation date is early 2012. when you open it its the color of beefboullion.will not desolve in hot water.returned to shpr

low in carbs and sugar free this is the besttasting sugarfree chocolate i have ever had. no weird aftertaste and definitely a cut above american chocolate. though i am not usually a candy eater because it hurts my teeth this definitely doesnt bother me. i wont say it is guiltfree at 170 calories but dark bar with hazelnuts and almonds was intensely satisfying. one a day will do me. i found these at a costco roadshow and will buy them again at amazon.
you really couldnt ask for better dried pineapple. sulferdioxide free and no additives at all. they are deliciously sweet and irresistible. if dried pineapple is your thing get these.
these chips are so addicting. let me tell you how addicting they are they are so addicting that whoops i used up my 20 word minimum already.addicting!

the design is enticing and it is moe readily availiable and affordable than island oasis. the taste is.... not appealing to me. it is just too sweet! but its an artificial sweet like jelly with sugar. it doesnt taste fresh. this stuff is still in the fridge. not my cup of tea..
i really wish that i could review this item but i just paid for no reason.

after trying soooo many powders we did settle on this one as being pleasing to the whole family.one of the problems weve had with a lot of the other protein powders is constipation especially garden of life holy cow!the taste of this product in middleoftheroad their not going to win any awards. but if you add stuff banana nut butter etc. i dont think the taste will be a deterrent. as with all hemp powders it is a tad gritty.nothing to write home about but overall1 my family prefers this one2. i am good with the product and ingredients3. its affordable

we have purchased these gummi bears a number of times over the past couple years. they are by far the best gummis that i have purchased and they are loved by my staff. excellent flavors always very freshstay fresh for 2 months in ziplock bags and are an amazing deal.we have had12 flavorpineappleblue raspberrypeachsourall have been excellent and are worth trying far better quality than haribo in my opinion.
after trying many evoos were convinced this is the best.this is our 2nd gallon! someone should have told us this stuff was addictive.

spices command a pretty penny. i typically buy large containers of spice from costco. costco has a very limited line and after running out of ginger i was blown away by the supermarket prices for ginger typically five dollars for an ounce to an ounce and a half. i was very pleased to find this product. i use ginger frequently and you get twice as much product for what you would spend at the supermarket. a low price doesnt mean much if the flavor is poor. i was happy with the flavoring. it yielded the flavor profile that i wanted. im happy that the company sells other options like cilantro which is a highcost dried herb. if you cook a lot with spices and herbs i would consider this company.note if you want to save even more money order directly from the company. google marshalls creek spices to find more spices from this company. the ginger on their website is under five dollars for six ounces but there is a higher shipping cost. so this option is probably better for those that want several bottles of spices.
summary: the taste is good and you cannot beat the price

text: the pups loved thesewell theyre peanut butter so who wouldnt love them? they went through the package of 7 bones in about 3 daysfighting and growling over them as if they were protecting a fresh killed antelope on the serengeti.

dont waste your money even more importantly your time. they leak badly dont seal so water just shoots through the cup not giving any time for coffee to brew. grounds in the coffee. a terrible waste of time and money.go back to ez filters.
the flavor when mixed with water is very weak plus the grams of protein are low compared to brands like designer whey and pure protein. i will not reorder this product.
summary: okay but there are better brands out there

text: our cat only ate 9 lives wet and dry. then one day she started vomitting. and it wouldnt stop. after every meal i would find surprises all around the house. mind you... i could care less about my cat its my husbands and the cat and i hate each other but i actually felt bad for her. so i researched and tried other kinds and then came across natural balance. she loves it! wet and dry and no vomitting!!! its a little expensive but cheeper than the vet or tons of carpet cleaner. i just wish more stores sold it so i wouldnt have to drive so far to pick it up. her coat is shiny and no dandruff. i dont remember the last time she vommitted. and she even seems to be acting better nicer since the new food. it did wonders on her!

funny how this product seems so polarizing. i dislike fig newtons but love these bars truefruit sweetness and gooey density. some bars do stick together. the filling is by no means hard to chew or gigantic though as some reviewers have oddly implied. if freshness was ever a problem it may have been corrected the shipment i got was several months before the expiration date.my only qualm though naturally sweetened each piece contains 8g sugar. the serving size of 2 bars is more filling than youd expect but if eaten as a quick breakfast or even lasting snack youd need at least 4 for 32g sugar. natural or not thats too much sugar.
medium blend with perfect amount of pumpkin. reminds me of starbucks piumpkin latte. half the price and more mild blend.
summary: delicious taste one fall in a cup

text: there are few dinners easier and more satisfying than a coated pork chop. for those that dont want the hassle and calories of frying shake n bake and similar coatings have been a staple for years. just an egg wash coat it with the mix and bake. its just that easy and just that good.however after trying both i would still have to give the nod to oven fry extra crispy pork coating. the snb is a finer more powdery texture whereas the oven fry gives it a thicker more flavorful crust that keep juices in better. oven fry gets a five from me snb only a three. but either way you go its better than a naked chop.
summary: second best coating for a pork chop

text: ive bought other brands here but decided to save some money and chose roland. im sorry to say they were not very good. the best so far have been byzantine peloponnese and barnier.
these seeds have a very peculiar flavor even after toasting. i do not recommend.

i use this soup as part of my daily effort to control my type 2 diabetes. it is tasty and filling and doesnt spike my blood sugar. for a little extra treat i add a little more grilled chicken and some crispy red or jalapeno peppers. love it so much i have 24 cans automatically shipped every month and save money over supermarket prices!
summary: tasty and filling low on carbs

text: i love these nut bars i wouldnt consider them granola bars really...more nuts than granola. these are great for travel because they dont get smashed.

i dont normally eat stuff like this but surprised the wife and kids with them and zap....they were gone. i managed to try one and it was moist and delicious and chocoriffic. these are a great idea to kick up the school lunches a notch.

honestly if you want an excellent pecan piefind a decent recipe make your own dough put fresh ingredients in it and make it yourself. otherwise the dough is going to taste stale and the filling mediocre. i have found no exceptions to this rule except a company called the grand traverse city pie company out of michigan that makes pies as good as homemade and no i am not in any way connected with them. and they do not sell online or by mail.

followed the recipe mai tais which stated to add additional lemon juice i believe...way to sour..check it first to see if it fits your needs and then mix accordingly. a good mix to have on hand to doctor other recipes.
this coffee is one of the best for basic morning coffee. it is bold without being bitter. i would definitly buy it again

for previous reviewer please note that the product details are for a 12 can case of 13 ounce cans. this product was recommended by my fabulous trusted vet of 30 years for my 15 year old cockerpoo buster. buster chows down happily prefers the chicken to beef whatever hes a trim and active dog even at this late stage of his life and i am sure the mature dog formula helps. people are surprised to hear that he is 15 years old and comment that he looks very fit for an older dog. if i could change anything about the product id have them substitute the corn for brown rice i do have this resentment to corn filler in so many products but dont let me get going on my environmentalagribusiness rant. its good stuff very reasonably priced and i dont have to lug the cases up from my car since it is delivered to my door!

these crackers are definitely a healthy snack but they dont taste exactly like what you think of when you think about a graham cracker. they are softer and dont have that crisp snap to them. they are small. they do help fillup your sweet tooth which is what i got them for. they have a little sweetness to them but arent sugary. they are ok overalla good snack for young children.
summary: healthy but not your typical tasting graham cracker

text: i used this in a cooked dish that i make. i soaked them first and found that they tasted okay. they dont work as well as fresh celery but they do work in a bind.

really like this snack bar but you really do have to like allot of nuts in your snack bars. we found there were just a few too many. but if you like nuts this bar is for you. high recommend this aussie product.

this is an absolute must for aloe vera drink lovers. i am so health conscious and in addition to the myriad of benefits of drinking aloe vera internally this particular formula is the best that i have ever tasted. first it is sweetened with cane sugar just like my boylan cream sodas and it has a hint of real grape flavor which makes it go down real easy! you will be pleased with yourself for this great tasting and 100 healthy amazing drink.

i did not purchase product from vendor listed i went to soupsonline website and ordered individual one. i really like to make homemade ham and bean soup and to me this base gives my soup that extra something in flavor that is needed.we like great northern beans but navy beans work too or any kind of bean you prefer.measure out 2 cups and sort out bad beans or rocks then soak over night.next day throw out old water put beans in a crock pot on high with enough ham base broth follow directions on jar to cover about 1 above the beans.add in cut up carrots potatoes celery and onions...if you dont like any of those veggies you can leave out any or all.i add a nice meaty ham hock because the meat is softer but be warned its a fattier part of the pig and cook until about 1 or so hours before done then take out the leg and shred meat from the bone and remove any fat and place back into pot. you can also use plain ham bits.i think it takes about 8 hrs or so to cook ie beans are nice and soft and so are the other veggies.this tastes good on the first day but makes even better leftovers as beans breakdown thicken even more and flavors meldthe very best thing about this recipe is that if you want your soup thinner add more water or ham base broth if you like your soup thicker start with amount i suggested.i make this soup often in the winter because it has stick to your bone goodness.this can also be used to help flavor green beans...

eight oclock decaf whole bean coffee is the best decaf weve ever drank. it was recommended by a total stranger who saw us purchasing our cuisinart coffee maker that grinds the whole beans. i cannot speak to their already ground decaf but the whole bean decaf is excellent. we do not like most decaf coffee just doesnt taste right but we cannot tell a flavor difference in the eight oclock whole bean decaf and regular coffee. highly recommend if youre looking for a great tasting decaf!

this kind of tastes like ice cream. unfortunately the similarities end there.the creamy texture that makes real ice cream feel good in your mouth must have been sucked out of the package along with the moisture back at the factory.biting down on this stuff is like biting into a hardened piece of neopolitanflavored insulating foam. i found it to be a pretty gross sensation and had a hard time finishing the bar.still its worth trying once!
summary: novelty food is not always novel food

text: i have loved emerils coffee for years. i ordered 4 boxes which arrived a few days ago. the kcups taste insipid with no aroma or flavorjust plain lousy.since amazon does not accept returns on foods i am stuck with 4 boxes of virtually undrinkable coffee. the box number is 1047. on the kcup is 0557pl26l4hope you have not ordered it.
summary: quality control has become a problem

text: i have tryed all the dog food at the stores and this is the only one she likes. could not find it any where so that is way i ordered it. thank you very much
since the grocery stores dont seem to carry this i checked here for it and glad i did. this stuff is awesome.

i cant live without aromat. ive grown up with it in switzerland and was able to buy it at safeway for years. they have discontinued however selling it and now i buy it in bulk online. still the best all rounder on the market!

i have eaten kopali organic mangos from grocery stores like whole foods before and they were excellent. the ones i received were horrible. i had different members of my family try them and they felt the same way. then when i tried to return the remaining packages amazon could not find my account and by the time it was straighten out it was too late to return them. i will not be purchasing food items from amazon again.

i love cinnamon and i regularly eat raisin bran crunch so i was looking forward to trying this cereal. for the most part i was disappointed. the flakes are much thinner than the crunch flakes. id equate them to the consistency of grape nuts flakes. the flavor was okay but id prefer something more substantial. i would have liked a stronger cinnamon taste too but as its easy for me to add more that didnt bother me too much. the real killer for me was that there were so few almonds. i got only a couple of them in each of my first two bowls. once i removed the inner wrapper from the box i could see that they really skimped on the quantity of almonds. so even ignoring my preference for a thicker flake considering that they went too light on the cinnamon for my taste and really skimped on the almonds i cant see ever buying this brand.
i have a modern kitchen and this looks perfect on the black granite matches all stainlesschrome appliances it holds plenty of kcups and spins effortlessly nice solid base and feels really well made
product description is not complete and clear. i ordered the wrong product. however amazon customer service is excellent and i got my refund after a phone call.

this is a tasty cereal and i really appreciate and love that it is organic. however it has a weird lingering taste and smell that i cant really describe...sort of plasticy. its not enough to dissuade me from eating it or buying it again but just something to note. also its really too bad that the puffs arent shaped like pandas. i wish they were.

text: arrowroot was recommended to us as a paleo alternative to cornstarch. we have used it in an amazing pecan pie as well as various soups and sauces. a great addition to our pantry!

my dog loves these and i have always bought them at sams club for 11. exact same package. i also buy them and donate them to the spca where i volunteer. they are a wonderful treat for the poor surrendered dogs there. however the price increase from sams club .is ridiculous. i will not puchase them again. hope this company will reconsider pricing and i am sure the volume increase in purchases would increase their profits even at the lower costs.

the taste for these is okay to me but they are really really dry and crumbly. they are so dry parts of them always get stuck in the back of my mouth when i eat them. if you are like me and prefer brownies a little mushy and moist you will not like these. sure they have natural ingredients but they are not much healthier than other better brownies on the market. if you are gonna eat a brownie eat a brownie for gods sake! they just arent meant to be anywhere close to health food.

ive never been a big fan of palak spinach. however the major problem with this item is that the palak gravy is too thin and the paneer tastes really bad.to make matters worse the sodium level in this product is ridiculously high. i bought this product thinking that it would be slightly more healthy compared to other packedfood products but the highsodium level ruined it.that said some of the other products by this brand for example dal bukhara and rajma taste much better. and if you are looking for healthy food i guess you should be looking somewhere else. buying some fresh vegetables at the local grocery store might be a good start in that regard.

i have been buying the 100 calorie packets of almonds and walnuts and came across these on amazon. i had not seen these in the stores and was surprised i hadnt. so i purchased some from amazon and they are really really good. a nice even mixture of almonds and walnuts coated in a sugar and cinnamon glaze with a hint of apple flavoring. yum!! the packet is not small like the 100 calorie packs and this is a great pick me up in the afternoon to tide me over until dinner. a perfect size to put in your lunch or purse or even a gym bag. i plan on buying more of the walnuts and almonds with the apple cinnamon glaze.
i own a senseo coffee maker and was delighted to find another brand of coffer that fits in this maker. great cup of coffee from a terrific supplier.

ive noticed that with a lot of products on the market today cereals and snack items like these soynuts the quality of the food is increasingly subpar i.e. there are almost always a few grains or other itemsdetritutus in the package that should not be there. a few of these beans for example look like they were dried w tiny black spots from dirt or perhaps insects? some might attribute this to the fact that these beans are a product of china and while i do not think that its origins are the primary source of the problem it might explain in part the low quality of the edamame. these green babies are tasty enough although the punch of the wasabi is low. texture is reminiscent of freeze dried food but more solid. for me the sodium level is just about right but i happen to believe that most products are too salty and that high sodium levels may have as much to do with americans expanding waist lines as hfcs. but back to the product at hand would i order these again? maybe but i am more careful about each bean i pop into my mouth now. as a highly allergy sensitive individual i cant afford to down bad beans no matter how tiny the black dots are.

dont get me wrong i love the sodastream and am especially happy with the cola flavors as they are very similar to cocacola products which was a deal breaker if they werent but i was very disappointed by the fountain mist and diet fountain mist mixes as they taste nothing like mountain dew as sodastream advertises. aside from not tasting like mountain dew it just doesnt taste very good overall. they dropped the ball on this one. but so far i am happy with all the other flavors. just skip this one.

well this would make a beautiful gift thats for sure.nicely packaged pretty foil packets.now the coffee so far weve only tried one of them. if that is anyindicator of the rest of the coffees this was a big waste of money.we just made a pot of the dessertsounding turtle sundae.gross! yes thats all i can say about it. the coffee tastedlike burnt cocoa like you tried to make hot chocolate left out the milk and used waterand let it boil dry on the stove. i tried adding creamer. still bad.i sure hope some of the other flavors are better. ill update my reviewas we try the other flavors but i dont hold out much hope right now.this was a christmas package but sealed in foil it should still be fresh enoughto taste good. if this was their best going in to the foil then avoid at all costs.
summary: nice packaging but the coffee is lacking

text: this oil leaves my hair very moisterized my hair is healthier i deep condition with this and seal with it as well. the best on the planet for sure!

i opened the snack cabinet in the office today and found something new sun maid vanilla yogurt raisins. i thought they might be a moderately healthy supplement to my boring lunch so i opened a box and popped a few in my mouth. the raisins were plumper than the normal desiccated sun maid product but the yogurt coating was sweet but not distinctive tasting and had a weirdly slick texture after being chewed a couple times. noting this i idly checked the ingredients while i waited for my tea to warm up in the microwave. natural california raisins check. yogurt coating... with partially hydrogenated palm kernel oil. hmm. the obverse of the box yielded the devilish nutrition fact i was looking for one box of these demons contains 4 grams of saturated fat or 20 of the recommended daily value.these things are moderately tasty but for that amount of saturated fat i want to cram something absolutely delicious in my cake hole like cake?. i would not recommend these to anyone and i would definitely never give them to my children thinking they were some kind of healthy snack.

lovely tea. this price is just absurd though. i will not be returning it simply due to the hassle but will not be ordering again due to the cost. if youre a tea lover then indulge but if youre looking for a bargain enjoy mt pick it up there...

my coffee machine is a grinder so i love the fresh beans. it smells good when its brewing and the taste is rich and not acidy. i got 25 buy 3 get the 4th free so it wound up being less expensive than if i purchased it here and i got it delivered right to my door. someone else rated the coffee with only one star not based on the coffee but on the price she paid. i think thats an unfair rating and not reflective of the products quality. you dont have to buy it if you think its over priced but if you check your prices and buy it right its better than most of what else is out there.

ive been using splenda for years. i didnt think this new splenda with b vitamins tasted any different than regular splenda.i guess its nice to have the added benefit of b vitamins. this splenda with b vitamins to help support a healthy metobolism does supply 20 of b vitamins b1 b5 and b6 per packet. i wish it also included b12 but it doesnt. that is a fair amount of b vitamins from one packet. i guess you really cant ask for anymore from one packet of a no calorie sweetener.the box has 80 packets which is kind of odd because i buy the regular splenda in a box of 100 packets. not sure why this variety only has 80 packets as the packets arent any larger than the regular splenda. each packet sweetens like 2 teaspoons of sugar. i added one packet to one large cup of hot tea. it was just sweet enough not to sweet.the splenda essentials line also comes in a fiber variety as well as an antioxidant variety. i do like that this comes in separate packets to take on the go as some restaurants do not have splenda.iam trying to cut down on my sugar intake due to an increase in my glucose level. i have to say that splenda is the most natural tasting no calorie sweetener. i have tried stevia but it has a weird taste to me.i like that you can use splenda in a hot or cold beverage as regular sugar doesnt desolve in a cold beverage.i really dont eat cereal with milk or fruit with sugar but iam sure this splenda would work for that too and foods like that.if you are trying to cut down on your sugar intake for whatever reason this splenda with b vitamins might be a good sugar alternative for you. or try one of the other varieties of splenda essentials.
summary: no difference between this and regular splenda in taste

ive been switching my cats over to healthier food and its been a struggle. they love purina indoor formula but any dry food other than that? they refuse to eat. after trying a few different healthy brands ive settled on this one.its a pebbled hard food thats probably really tough on an older cats teeth so fair warning. its also smelly as heck. it smells a bit like wheaty cardboard and is the same color. my cats turned their noses up to this for days and i worried that they wouldnt eat it. now they eat it albeit reluctantly. its the cat equivalent of broccoli or brussel sprouts. its good for them but theyre not enjoying it.ive been giving them the indoor health recipe for about a month now and they eat it but very slowly and pickily. i supplement their diet with wellness core canned food which they love and will stick with this a bit longer. it does make a difference in their coats. i have one cat that has dry skin and her coat has been a lot healthier and with no dry skin flakes so thats a success. hairballs have gone down to almost nothing too.i just wish they enjoyed it more!

very consistent product pours uniformly easily but is extremely salty0 for goiter prevention! 5 for valuetaste! overallmorton noniodized salt is a lot like mortons iodized salt and cocacola it is available everywhere and you can be sure of what you are getting. morton makes salt into their reason for being so we can be pretty sure that they get it right. every container of mortons iodized salt that i have used over the past 50 years was exactly like every other which is the kind of consistency that makes a consumer feel safe. if you want to avoid goiter however this is not the ticket. instead you will need to use mortons iodized salt and thats an example of how a tiny amount of a trace mineral can go a long way. but iodized salt just like this plain salt must be taken in very small doses and only when it must be added to something you are cooking.at mortons very informative website which will not copy here but it goes by there name there are links to useful nutritional websites such as the salt institute. i know what you are thinking but it does have wonderful color slides of human anatomy showing how much salt is in our body and what it does. essentially salt is an essential mineral that is overconsumed in processed foods. however if you dont eat processed foods you will need salt and iodine to be added to what you eat especially if you exercise daily or if you live in a desert or other hot place.this is a product iodized version not this one that my family buys once every 3 years so how could we ever consider saving a dime with a store brand when we have 50 years of satisfied experience with mortons iodized salt? i love the little girl on the packaging also. she hasnt changed much since 1914 and not at all since 1968. some things cant be improved just like mortons salt.please note unless you are allergic to iodine which is almost unheard of you should be using the mortons iodized salt to prevent goiter and not this plain salt product.not affiliated with any company or product.
summary: great salt without iodine added why?

in rescue i see some pretty sad and sickly thin coated dogs. i rescue primarily small dogs. i cant sing the praises enough about this avoderm dog food. my 9 yr. old senior 4 lb. longhair rescue chihuahua went from being the skinniest little thing with bare paws and a thin coat to being a 5 lb. stunning fur muff on avoderm. she doesnt have teeth so i get her the chicken with veggies canned and i mix in a little bit of water to make it easier for her to eat. she absolutely loves it and gobbles it up and it clearly loves her cause shes absolutely gorgeous now. i wish i could show you before and after pictures. you wouldnt believe the difference the avoderm made! all my rescue dogs eat it and the avoderm kibble and do fabulously well and develop muscles strong bones and shiny coats on it.

we have an almost 2 year old female pug. we had tried several dog foods before such as purina fit and trim kibbles n bits and even tried the royal canin food specifically for pugs. they all had corn as well as other fillers near the top of the list. she would eat the food of course cause shes a pug but most days it would just sit in there and then randomly she would eat it little by little. you could just tell she didnt really enjoy it. we decided to try out some of the blue buffalo dog food and wow she loves it. she gets excited when its food time and eats the food immediately. and it also gives us peace of mind that she is getting excellent ingredients that will help her be healthier. its more expensive than most of the store bought brands but knowing you are giving your little dog the best food is worth it. highly recommended.

i first discovered this product in our local food lion grocery store but they stopped carrying them. i searched everywhere because my 3 12 year old granddaughter loved them. i am trying to offer her snacks that are healthful and she is exposed to so much junk. i looked online and found them. i bought 2 packs of 2 different flavors and she was so excited that her gran bought them just for her! i highly recommend them. you can always keep the excess quantity in the freezer until you need them.very happy grandmother.

i tried this for the first time. i was attracted to it because it claimed it was 99 digestable and did not pose the chokingblockage hazard that rawhide does.my dog loved it but it did not last long. he consumed it within 3 minutes. then about 2030 minutes later we had a big problem. he went into a seizure both of his rear legs locked up he was flailing his front legs around trying to get back up his heart was pounding he was panting heavily had his teeth clenched he was heavily salivating and he defacated. he finally started to act relaxed and then his eyes lost all life i thought i was watching my dog die in front of me.luckily this event subsided and he went back to normal. about 30 minutes after the seizure he had a full bowel movement. later he ate a regular dinner and had another regular bowel movement so that told me it wasnt a blockage. he is only 3 yrs old and strong as an ox 85 lb collie i have my doubts that it could be epilepsy or a brain tumor. i think he had a severe allergic reaction to the kelp and chlorophyl contained in the snack or possibly that particular bag was tainted somehow. the sellby date was good. it was highly coincidental that this all happened so shortly after feeding him this snack. i threw the rest of the snacks away and i will never buy them again.
summary: my dog had a severe allergic reaction

when searching for a dog food i read many articles about the importance of not having corn or meat byproducts in a dogs food due to the sensitive nature of a dogs stomach. some of these articles would come from dog food websites where the ingredient list would have corn as the first ingredient. we originally bought the wellness brand puppy food but shortly after we got our dog we realized that we were going to need food for a large breed puppy. luckily wellness makes that food. it contains no corn and we have a happy energetic puppy with a shiny coat and clear eyes. she never leaves a bite in her dish!
